scope: bodykit only; Unitree R1 base robot, freight, duties, and operator labor are excluded unless explicitly listed

current_bodykit_evidence.volume_note: The current mesh volume is a solid proxy from EVT shells. Production shells must be hollowed and rebuilt with wall thickness, ribs, bosses, and split lines before RFQ.

source_references.name: Unitree R1 official shop
source_references.used_for: base robot price and shipping assumptions

source_references.name: Shapeways PA12
source_references.used_for: SLS PA12 bounding boxes, wall guidance, and prototype print process

source_references.name: Protolabs injection molding cost/design guidance
source_references.used_for: mold cost drivers, part-size constraints, and DFM planning

source_references.name: Xometry injection molding service
source_references.used_for: aluminum versus steel tooling and production service path

source_references.name: Hubs injection molding guide
source_references.used_for: family molds, cost drivers, and injection molding DFM guidance

source_references.name: Fictiv injection molding services
source_references.used_for: bridge production, aluminum and steel mold sourcing path

source_references.name: FACEHEAD.CO
source_references.used_for: custom animatronic face and sculpture supplier benchmark

source_references.name: Silicone Mask Laboratory
source_references.used_for: custom silicone mask and robot skin benchmark

prototype: FDM ASA or PETG for first physical fit
SLS or MJF PA12 for cleaner functional prototype panels
SLA or high-resolution resin only for face masters and small inserts
production: PC-ABS or ASA for glossy orange armor shells
Black PC-ABS or ABS for underbody, sole, and detail inserts
Painted hard plastic face shell for the current no-hair bodykit path

volume_cost_matrix.recommended_process: FDM ASA/PETG prototype plus filler, sanding, paint, and hand-fit hardware
volume_cost_matrix.notes: Best path for one-off chassis fit and aesthetic review. Not production-grade surface finish without substantial labor.

volume_cost_matrix.recommended_process: SLS/MJF PA12 pilot run or bridge tooling for selected repeat parts
volume_cost_matrix.notes: Use for pilot builds after the bodykit passes production clearance. Paint and hand assembly still dominate.

volume_cost_matrix.recommended_process: aluminum or mixed aluminum/steel injection tooling with family tools where DFM allows
volume_cost_matrix.notes: Likely crossover range for molded shells if the design is frozen and common materials/colors are family-tooled.

volume_cost_matrix.recommended_process: production steel tooling, multi-cavity or family tools, molded color plus selective paint
volume_cost_matrix.notes: Requires finished production CAD, steel-safe DFM, QA fixtures, packaging, and supplier-managed color/texture control.

face_head_sourcing_options.fit: best for open-source hard-plastic bodykit; requires production CAD rebuild of current donor surface

face_head_sourcing_options.path: custom silicone mask or robot skin supplier
face_head_sourcing_options.fit: use only if the project shifts from hard-plastic mask to soft expressive skin
benchmark or premium option; not aligned with current hard-plastic shell scope unless the head becomes a separate purchased module

rfq_package_required_before_quotes: 2D critical dimensions and tolerance drawings for mounting features
clearance report passing static and dynamic gates
assembly BOM with fasteners, inserts, magnets, adhesive, and paint masks
rendered appearance target and color/texture chips
open_blockers: production clearance verdict is still needs-work
no current bodykit parts are blocked from STEP export
part review verdict is still needs-work
current face shell is a donor-derived parametric grid loft requiring surface-class review and final production DFM
current cost numbers are planning ranges and require supplier RFQ
